channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Tue, 19 May 2026 15:07:17 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: UAE to launch 16 new Indian passport and visa centres as Alhind takes over consular services
item.description: Over four million Indians in the UAE will experience a significant overhaul in passport and visa services starting July 1, 2026. Kerala-based Alhind Group has secured a major contract, launching 16 new service centers across the Emirates. This move aims to make services more affordable and accessible, replacing BLS International after a decade.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ebola outbreak worries WHO over 'scale and speed': What to know
item.description: A severe Ebola outbreak, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, has claimed 130 lives with over 500 suspected cases. The World Health Organization warns of high risk to neighboring nations, stressing human-to-human transmission via bodily fluids and contact with infected animals. Emergency response systems are being activated to combat the escalating crisis.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘They kicked me like an animal’: Israeli tourist speaking Hebrew attacked in London
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: SpaceX prepares to launch Starship Version 3 rocket: Countdown begins for May 20 launch of upgraded mega rocket
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: UAE brings in unified payday deadline for private sector workers from June 2026 under new wage rules
item.description: UAE mandates private firms to pay staff by the first of each Gregorian month from June 1, 2026. This stringent wage protection reform aims to bolster worker rights and labour compliance. The move standardizes payroll, with delayed payments automatically flagged, impacting millions of expatriates reliant on timely salaries for essential expenses.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How this Indonesian island was created by a volcano that destroyed itself
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 25th visit to China: Xi Jinping’s ‘old friend' Putin to arrive on Tuesday - what's on agenda
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is gearing up for a major two-day sojourn in China, coinciding with the backdrop of a US presidential visit. His face-to-face meeting with 'old friend' President Xi Jinping aims to fortify their countries' bilateral relations and expand collaborative strategies. Topics will span a range of regional and international challenges, with the prospect of new pacts emerging.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This ancient bullet carried a secret 2,100-year-old Greek word that shocked experts worldwide revealing ‘sarcastic’ war message
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: World’s longest hiccup case ever recorded: The man who hiccupped more than 430 million times over 68 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Experts stunned by a forgotten medieval book in Rome hiding oldest English poem
item.description: Researchers in Ireland have unearthed the oldest known English poem, "Caedmon’s Hymn," dating back to the 7th century. This remarkable discovery, found embedded within a 9th-century Latin manuscript in a Roman library, predates previous earliest known copies by three centuries, offering new insights into the early spread of the English language.

item.title: Buried for 3,000 years: Egypt’s Lost Golden City of Luxor reveals an ancient world frozen in time
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Iran plans €50mn bounty on 'gambler' Trump, 'child-killer' Netanyahu: Report
item.description: Iran's parliament is considering hefty financial rewards for assassinating US President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu, escalating tensions after recent strikes. Lawmakers aim to formalize bounties, viewing these leaders as responsible for the death of Supreme Leader Khamenei. This move shifts threats from rhetoric to official parliamentary action, with warnings of wider retaliation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Cuba: US military action would lead to a ‘bloodbath’
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel has issued a stark warning about the potential fallout from U.S. military actions, predicting a 'bloodbath' and significant turmoil across the region. He emphasized that Cuba is not a threat to its neighbors. This statement arises in light of rumors surrounding Cuba’s supposed procurement of over 300 military drones, coupled with plans to attack U.S. interests.

item.title: Growing furniture: This UK couple is growing actual chairs from living trees
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: DOJ announces $1.8bn fund to compensate Trump allies
item.description: The Trump administration has rolled out the 'AntiWeaponisation Fund', a staggering $1.8 billion initiative aimed at reconciling claims from allies of the former president, who contend they have faced unjust scrutiny from the Justice Department. This fund is a direct result of Trump’s decision to drop his extensive $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS, signaling a shift in strategy.

item.title: Vax crisis inherited, had no measles shots in stock when we took office: BNP govt
item.description: Bangladesh faces a severe measles crisis with hundreds of child deaths and thousands infected. The current government claims it inherited a complete lack of vaccines, blaming previous administrations for the outbreak due to halted immunization programs. Authorities are now racing to vaccinate, while the High Court considers barring officials from leaving the country amid investigations into vaccine shortages.
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: Pak sends new Iranian peace proposal to US
item.description: Iran has initiated a bold peace proposal to the United States, channeled through Pakistan, in a bid to halt the West Asia conflict and prioritize the lifting of maritime sanctions. Talks on nuclear issues would be postponed under this new agreement.

item.title: Texas whistleblower asks how H-1Bs live in beautiful, $800,000 houses, blames Biden for giving out loans
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Riot Games removes Arcane song “Remember Me” featuring D4vd amid first-degree murder case
item.description: Riot Games has reportedly pulled the Arcane soundtrack song 'Remember Me,' featuring artist D4vd, from some official music platforms. This action follows the singer's recent first-degree murder charges in Los Angeles. While the song remains accessible on some platforms, its removal from official Riot channels suggests a response to reputational concerns amid ongoing legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Active shooter at Islamic Center of San Diego, nearby elementary school evacuated
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
